pubmed_links: [Wang et al. (2024) Shared genetic basis and causality between schizophrenia and inflammatory bowel disease](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38563283/);[Delacrétaz et al. (2017) Association of variants in SH2B1 and RABEP1 with worsening of low-density lipoprotein and glucose parameters in patients treated with psychotropic drugs](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28694205/);[Novikova et al. (2021) Integration of Alzheimer's disease genetics and myeloid genomics identifies disease risk regulatory elements and genes](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33712570/)
summary: RABEP1 (rabaptin-5), a RAB GTPase effector involved in endosomal membrane trafficking, has been identified as a pleiotropic gene shared between schizophrenia and inflammatory bowel disease via cross-trait GWAS, and as a candidate causal gene in Alzheimer's disease risk loci through myeloid enhancer regulation; a variant in RABEP1 (rs1000940) has also been directly associated with altered fasting glucose in psychiatric patients receiving psychotropic medications. (novel candidate)
